corpus-viewer-build.outcome: Turn apps/corpus-viewer from a macOS-only SwiftUI browser into a dual-platform (macOS + iOS) app that browses AND searches the vendor/docker + vendor/xcode markdown corpus, runnable on the iPhone 17 Pro simulator. Render via MarkdownUI; outline + search via swift-markdown's AST. No model parses pages at runtime — the app is deterministic Swift.

boundaries.directive: Dispatch only TaskEnvelopes. A subagent receives a typed `input` and MUST return an object validated against the schema named in `outputSchemaRef`. No prose crosses the boundary; a malformed reply is a FAILED task.
Accept a subagent result only after parseOutput() validates it. The verifier gate (buildVerifierGate) lets a task be marked done only on a `pass` (or an explicitly-allowed `warn`).
One foreground task per subagent at a time. Fan out across DIFFERENT subagents freely; never run two foreground tasks on the same worker.

budgets.directive: Each subagent has a TokenBudget (maxOutputTokens, compactAtInputTokens, maxTurns). Refuse to dispatch a task whose budget exceeds the subagent's ceiling (assertWithinBudget).
Consult TurnLedger.stopReason() each turn. On "output-budget" or "turns", STOP and return the last structured result with truncated=true.

model-routing.directive: Design-heavy work (swift-porter, doc-indexer, build-verifier) runs on claude-opus-4-8 with high effort + adaptive thinking. Mechanical work (ios-bundler: rsync + Package.swift edits) runs on haiku, low effort, thinking disabled — do not burn thinking tokens on file copies.
Adaptive thinking is the only thinking-on mode on Opus 4.8. Never request a fixed thinking budget for an opus-4-8 agent.

caching.directive: Split each dispatch into a STABLE cached prefix (subagent system prompt + shared Swift templates + tool definitions) and a DYNAMIC suffix (this task). The prefix is identical across a subagent's tasks so the prompt cache reuses it. Tool definitions are cached (tool-use-with-prompt-caching).

When TurnLedger.shouldCompact() fires, compact the transcript to its last StructuredOutput + open next-steps. Append the compaction summary as a mid-conversation system message that PRESERVES the cache prefix.

memory.directive: Persist each subagent's load-bearing decisions to project memory (.claude/agent-memory//), capped to recent decisions so memory itself stays bounded. Read it back at the start of each task.

outcome-driven-citations.directive: Any field that encodes a documentation fact carries a Citation (source + last_fetched). Tests cite vendor/ docs; when a doc drifts, citation-guard + the freshness check fail loudly. This is how the build stays correct as docs go out of date.

Beta / research-preview surfaces (Agent SDK minor versions, adaptive thinking, dynamic workflows) are declared as BetaFeature objects so one test asserts they're acknowledged and version drift is caught.

tdd.directive: Tests come first and are the priority. Every module under src/agent/corpus-viewer/ has a sibling *.test.ts carrying an @cite header (enforced by scripts/lib/citation-guard.ts). A phase is not done until its tests pass AND `swift build` + the iOS simulator screenshot pass.
